_schema.name: short id used in results table
_schema.urls: map of transport → URL; keys: raw-quic, h3-wt
_schema.disabled: bool; if true, skip this relay entirely (default false)
_schema.disabled_suites: list of interop suite names to skip for this relay

relays.name: moqx-main
relays.notes: openmoq moqx CI relay. Covers drafts 14/16/18 over both raw-QUIC and H3/WT (/moq-relay).

relays.name: cloudflare-d14
relays.notes: CF d14 moq-rs: publisher must send PUB_NS + PUBLISH. relay-fetch disabled (#24): verified unsupported — cloudflare/moq-rs README lists FETCH 'Not Soon', and its relay neither forwards nor errors FETCH (open upstream issues cloudflare/moq-rs#57 passthrough, #58 error), so it never answers (TimeoutError). join works.

relays.name: moq-dev-anon
relays.notes: Subscriber-only anon endpoint: publish is rejected at QUIC (code 91141958510816), SUBSCRIBE returns non-spec code=404 Broadcast-not-found. Re-enable if a write-capable path becomes available.

relays.name: quichemoq
relays.notes: Every connect drops with bare ConnectionError (no QUIC code, no reason). Possibly not MoQT-speaking on this port. Re-probe before enabling.

relays.name: quicr-west
relays.notes: Cisco libquicr (server cert unacceptable → insecure). d14 dropped (raw + WT now unresponsive — relay appears d16-only). compat=libquicr covers two non-spec behaviors, tolerated + annotated: (1) relay returns SUBSCRIBE_OK for a nonexistent track (deferred-delivery policy); (2) relay returns FETCH_ERROR code=0 (INTERNAL_ERROR) for a nonexistent-track FETCH — the fetch probe honors libquicr code=0 as a deliberate refusal (the relay answered structurally in ~85ms; it just uses a poor code), matching how the join probe already accepted its non-spec code=17. Without --compat the fetch probe still fails code=0 by default (0.9.12 stance: 0x0 is not a refusal).

relays.name: moqtail
relays.notes: OzU moqtail (Rust), WT d14 only. Disabled: WT CONNECT refused (code=0) at the catalog URL (no :path). Re-enable once the correct WebTransport path is known / relay confirmed up.

relays.name: imquic
relays.notes: Meetecho imquic (C). d14 probes rejected (WT: "No WebTransport protocol offered"; QUIC close 376); only d16 tested here.

relays.name: cf-d16-interop
relays.notes: Cloudflare moq-rs draft-16 interop branch. compat=lenient-extensions: this fork emits a truncated trailing-extensions KVP block on SubscribeOk (kvp_start=6 tell=7 exts_end=7) which our strict parser rejects as non-compliant; tolerated + annotated, recovering ctrl-msg to 6/6. relay-join + relay-fetch disabled (#24): verified unsupported — itzmanish/moq-rs README also lists FETCH 'Not Soon', and the relay never answers JOIN/FETCH (TimeoutError; join is a joining-fetch). pub-sub[publish] kept as a genuine fail; root-caused 2026-06-21 to two layers: (A) [our side, FIXED] with pub_mode=publish-both the namespace resolves, but the relay's SubscribeOk carries the same truncated trailing-extensions block as its ctrl-msg path, which crashed the multi_sub_bench subscriber (the lenient-extensions tolerance lived only in moq_interop_client, not the bench) — now plumbed via `multi_sub_bench --compat lenient-extensions`; (B) [relay side, confirmed] even with control fully working and the publisher sending 300+ objects, the relay forwards ZERO to subscribers. Ruled out on our side via live tests + spec §10.1: forward=0 and forward=1, alias-matched and alias-diverged all give 0 objects, and the relay never RESETs our data streams (track_alias has no parity rule, only session-uniqueness, which we satisfy). With bare pub_mode=publish the surface symptom is code=4 'Track not found' (relay never got PUB_NS). The same flow delivers objects vs moxygen/imquic/cloudflare-d14, so (B) is a moq-rs d16-fork forwarding bug. Upstream also advertises an H3/WT endpoint at https://.../moq but our client hits a SETUP parse error ("Read out of bounds") — QUIC only here until that's resolved.

relays.name: fb-mvfst-d18
relays.notes: Upstream moxygen/mvfst reference at draft-18 — the conformant d18 reference (control 6/6 over raw-QUIC, 2026-06-22). Separate row from moxygen-fb, which stays d14/d16.

relays.name: nokia-d18
relays.notes: Nokia Research draft-18 relay (independent impl). ~5/6: announce-subscribe fails relay-side (the reference moq-dev-rs client also fails it there), not an aiomoqt issue.

relays.name: cf-d18-interop
relays.notes: Cloudflare moq-rs draft-18-dev. Setup OK; announce/subscribe fail relay-side — moq-rs draft-18-dev has no accept_bi, so it never reads the per-request bidirectional request streams IETF d18 §3 mandates (verified against a locally-built relay + mlog). Kept as a documented relay-side failure rather than masked.
